canonical_question: What is the smart contract security audit checklist (Solidity)?
aliases: how to audit a Solidity smart contract
Ethereum smart contract security best practices

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: Solidity 0.8.0 (Dec 2020) built-in overflow checks; EEA EthTrust Security Levels v2 (Dec 2023)

constraints: Automated tools catch only ~30-50% of vulnerabilities -- always combine with manual expert review
Solidity 0.8.x+ has built-in overflow protection; for 0.7.x and below, SafeMath is mandatory
Never deploy unaudited contracts that handle user funds -- immutability means bugs cannot be patched post-deployment
Audit the deployed bytecode, not just source -- compiler optimizations can introduce unexpected behavior
Re-audit after every significant code change -- even single-line changes can introduce critical vulnerabilities

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Auditing a Move or Rust/Solana smart contract, not Solidity/EVM
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Chain-specific audit guides (Anchor for Solana, Move Prover for Aptos/Sui)

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need a general web application security checklist, not blockchain-specific
Only need gas optimization, not security

inputs_needed.question: What Solidity compiler version is the contract using?
What type of contract are you auditing?
What level of audit are you performing?
options: Full audit (manual + static + fuzzing + formal verification)

sources.title: Ethereum Smart Contract Security Best Practices
sources.author: ConsenSys Diligence

sources.title: Slither -- Static Analysis Framework for Solidity
sources.author: Trail of Bits

sources.title: Mythril -- Security Analysis Tool for EVM Bytecode
